novel_features.name: Daily Standup Digest
novel_features.description: See your PRs awaiting review, in-progress work items, and newly failed builds in one command — ready before the standup meeting.
novel_features.rationale: Requires cross-entity aggregation across PRs, work items, and builds that only the local SQLite mirror can join in a single query.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs a quick briefing on a developer's current work state before creating tasks or PRs on their behalf.

novel_features.name: PR Review Readiness Queue
novel_features.description: See only the PRs where you are a required reviewer, the build is passing, and no other required reviewer has voted yet — ordered by readiness.
novel_features.rationale: Joins local PR reviewer/vote state against live build status; no API endpoint or existing tool combines these signals into a prioritized queue.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs to identify which PRs a developer should review first to unblock teammates.

novel_features.name: Sprint Velocity Trend
novel_features.description: See sprint-over-sprint velocity with trend line for the current team — points completed vs committed, across the last N sprints.
novel_features.rationale: Requires cross-sprint join of work_items + state_history + iterations tables in local SQLite; infeasible to compute live without O(N*M) API calls.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs to assess team capacity or forecast delivery dates based on historical throughput.

novel_features.name: Sprint Scope Creep Detector
novel_features.description: See which work items were added to the current sprint after it started, with total story points of scope added mid-sprint.
novel_features.rationale: Joins work_item_revisions (IterationPath timestamp changes) against the sprint start date — a cross-table query that no single ADO API provides.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs to identify whether a sprint is at risk due to scope expansion after commit.

novel_features.name: Sprint Rollover Counter
novel_features.description: Find work items that have been moved to a new sprint more than once — the chronic blockers that keep slipping through sprint planning.
novel_features.rationale: Aggregates IterationPath field changes across revision history; requires local revision storage to count across multiple sprints.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs to identify persistent delivery risks before sprint planning.

novel_features.name: Cross-Pipeline Pending Approvals
novel_features.description: See all release gates and YAML pipeline stages currently waiting for your approval across every pipeline, with how long each has been waiting.
novel_features.rationale: Merges the classic release approvals API and YAML pipeline approvals API — two different endpoint shapes that no existing CLI or MCP unifies.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs to identify which deployments are blocked on human approval and how urgent each is.

novel_features.name: Work Item Revision Diff
novel_features.description: Show exactly which fields changed between two revisions of a work item — a git-style diff for specification and acceptance criteria.
novel_features.rationale: Fetches two revision snapshots via /wit/workItems/{id}/revisions/{n} and diffs the field maps locally; the API returns raw snapshots but no diff surface.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs to understand what changed in a work item's requirements between two points in time.

novel_features.name: Pipeline Flakiness Score
novel_features.description: Identify build stages with the highest intermittent failure rate over the last N runs — the stages that fail and then pass without code changes.
novel_features.rationale: Joins build_history by pipeline definition and computes per-stage failure alternation rate; requires build history volume that is infeasible to compute from live APIs.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs to identify which pipeline stages are unreliable test signals before acting on build failures.

novel_features.name: Work Item Cycle Time
novel_features.description: Measure average time from Active to Done per work item type over a date range — the team's true delivery throughput.
novel_features.rationale: Joins work_item_revisions to compute Active→Done duration per item; revision history is prohibitively expensive to paginate live for trend analysis.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs to estimate realistic delivery timelines based on historical cycle time.

novel_features.name: Commit-to-Build Traceability
novel_features.description: Given a commit SHA, see every build and pipeline run that included that commit and whether it passed or failed.
novel_features.rationale: Joins local builds × build_changes tables on commit SHA; no ADO endpoint offers reverse-lookup 'builds that contain this commit'.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs to determine whether a specific code change has been validated by CI.

novel_features.name: Cross-Repo Branch Health
novel_features.description: See a health dashboard for every repo in the project: default branch build status, last commit age, and open PR count — in one table.
novel_features.rationale: Four-way SQLite join across repos, branches, builds, and PRs; no existing tool produces a cross-repo health view without clicking through each repo.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs to assess overall code health across a multi-repo project before planning changes.

novel_features.name: Area Path Work Item Load
novel_features.description: See open work item counts and total story points grouped by area path — to spot overloaded teams or abandoned backlogs.
novel_features.rationale: SQLite GROUP BY aggregation over synced work_items; WIQL cannot return group-by aggregations, only individual items.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs to identify which teams have the most outstanding work before assigning new items.

novel_features.name: PR Aging Report
novel_features.description: Find pull requests with no reviewer activity for N or more days, grouped by author — to surface forgotten PRs before they go stale.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-joins local PRs × timeline events to identify inactivity gaps; requires PR history that is expensive to retrieve and aggregate live.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs to identify PRs that are at risk of losing context due to review delays.

novel_features.name: Branch Stale Cleanup Plan
novel_features.description: List branches that are safe to delete: already merged into default, no open PRs, and no commits in the last N days.
novel_features.rationale: Joins synced branches × merged PRs × last-commit timestamps; no ADO API endpoint or existing CLI identifies candidates for deletion across all three criteria.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent is helping clean up a repository's branch proliferation.

novel_features.name: Build Cost Trend
novel_features.description: See agent minutes consumed per pipeline per week over the last N weeks — to find pipelines that are growing in CI cost.
novel_features.rationale: Joins agent_queue + build_history + duration data; ADO Analytics has the raw data but no CLI surfaces it as a weekly trend view.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs to identify which pipelines are contributing to growing CI/CD costs.

narrative.headline: The fastest Azure DevOps CLI — offline-first, agent-native, and the only one that answers 'why is this sprint burning' without opening a browser.
narrative.value_prop: azure-devops-pp-cli syncs your work items, builds, and PRs to a local SQLite mirror and lets you query them with SQL or structured --agent output. It matches everything `az devops` and the Microsoft MCP server can do, then adds fifteen cross-entity analytics commands that require no live API calls — sprint velocity, PR review queues, scope creep detection, cycle time analysis, and more.
narrative.auth_narrative: Set AZURE_DEVOPS_TOKEN to a Personal Access Token. Generate one at dev.azure.com/{org}/_usersSettings/tokens with scopes: Work Items (read/write), Code (read), Build (read), Release (read/execute). Run `azure-devops-pp-cli auth setup --launch` to open the token page. Save with `azure-devops-pp-cli auth set-token <your-pat>`.

quickstart.comment: Verify config and connectivity before syncing
Preview what standup will fetch once auth is configured
Morning digest: your PRs, work items, and failed builds
Which PRs need your review right now (build green + no votes yet)
Team velocity trend over the last 6 sprints

troubleshoots.fix: Check AZURE_DEVOPS_TOKEN is set and contains a valid PAT. Regenerate at dev.azure.com/{org}/_usersSettings/tokens.
Set AZURE_DEVOPS_PROJECT to the exact project name (case-sensitive). Run `azure-devops-pp-cli projects list` to see available projects.
Azure DevOps rate-limits at 200 TSTUs per 5 minutes. Add --max-pages 5 to limit sync volume, or wait 5 minutes and retry.

troubleshoots.symptom: standup returns empty results after sync
troubleshoots.fix: Run `azure-devops-pp-cli standup --dry-run` to verify the command resolves, then run without --dry-run once AZURE_DEVOPS_TOKEN, AZURE_DEVOPS_ORG, and AZURE_DEVOPS_PROJECT are set.

troubleshoots.symptom: auth set-token shows 'credential saved' but doctor still shows 'not configured'
troubleshoots.fix: Restart the terminal to clear any old AZURE_DEVOPS_TOKEN env var that overrides the saved credential. Run `azure-devops-pp-cli auth status` to see which source is active.

narrative.when_to_use: Use azure-devops-pp-cli when an agent needs to read or write Azure DevOps data: work items, pull requests, pipelines, builds, or sprint state. It is the fastest path to structured JSON output for ADO data without a browser or the Python-based az devops extension. Use it especially for cross-entity analytics (velocity, cycle time, scope creep) that require the local SQLite mirror. Prefer `az devops` only when you need features this CLI does not yet cover (migrations, live entitlement management).
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI for Azure resource management (VMs, storage, AKS) — use the `az` CLI instead.
Do not use this CLI for GitHub repositories — use the `gh` CLI.
Do not use this CLI for reading raw file content from Azure Blob Storage.
Do not use this CLI for Azure Boards queries that require real-time board state not yet synced.

recipes.title: Morning standup in one command
recipes.explanation: Returns your daily briefing as structured JSON: PRs waiting for your review, work items in progress, and builds that failed since yesterday.

recipes.title: Find PRs ready for your review
recipes.explanation: Shows only PRs where you are required, the build is green, and no other required reviewer has voted — ordered by most ready first.

recipes.title: Sprint velocity trend for capacity planning
recipes.explanation: Returns 8-sprint velocity history as structured JSON for an agent to compute averages and forecast next sprint capacity.

recipes.title: Scope creep check at sprint mid-point
recipes.explanation: Shows what was added to the current sprint after it started, with story points and percentage of total sprint scope that crept in.

recipes.title: Cross-repo health before a release
recipes.explanation: One-line summary of all repos: default branch build status, days since last commit, and open PR count — to confirm everything is green before a release.

trigger_phrases: show my Azure DevOps work items
